description: This pipeline processes images by first splitting multichannel images into individual greyscale channels, which are then further processed and filtered to extract specific information, such as maximum values after applying a Gaussian filter. The processed channels are then merged back together to form a composite image, with optional conversion of the image type during the process.

cfc5808b-552f-46b6-8534-15b391012c47.name: ij1-contrast-illumination-correction2d
cfc5808b-552f-46b6-8534-15b391012c47.description: Applies a Gaussian filter to the image and extracts the maximum value. Pixel values are then divided by this value.If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

66a0e38a-0a71-4973-bfb6-69b2a1516e1f.name: ij-convert-image
66a0e38a-0a71-4973-bfb6-69b2a1516e1f.description: Converts an image into another image type

54992ce8-f63d-4ff3-8976-47a1b4904844.name: ij1-color-split-channels-2
54992ce8-f63d-4ff3-8976-47a1b4904844.description: Splits multichannel images into multiple greyscale images. This operation is applied for each 2D image slice.

082caa50-7cfb-4bfe-98d2-f7700559eb02.name: ij1-color-merge-channels
082caa50-7cfb-4bfe-98d2-f7700559eb02.description:
Merges each greyscale image plane into a multi-channel image. Implements the standard ImageJ algorithm for merging channels that either creates an RGB or a composite image. We recommend to use Blend images for creating renders and Merge channels (composite) that provide more options for the specific use cases.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.
